key_insight: CPTabKAN combines physiologically grouped EEG concepts with polynomial interaction modeling to improve interpretable mild cognitive impairment detection.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a clinically usable, high-accuracy tabular learning workflow that preserves domain structure while exposing which EEG concepts drive decisions.
method: Map 1,379 EEG-derived features into 10 physiologically motivated concept groups.
Apply degree-2 polynomial expansion to capture first- and second-order cross-concept interactions.
Use a Fourier-parameterized Kolmogorov-Arnold tabular classifier for nonlinear classification and concept weighting.
evidence_signal: On the Study of Osteoporotic Fractures cohort (372 subjects), CPTabKAN-Second Order achieved a weighted F1-score of 0.9038 under 10-fold cross-validation, outperforming GradientBoosting by 5.65 points.
watch_item: The reported gains depend on cross-validation and cohort-specific EEG features, so external validation on independent clinical datasets is still needed.
paper_title: Interpretable Concept-Guided Polynomial Tabular Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for EEG-Based Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection
